  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" name="us-gaap:DebtDisclosureTextBlock" id="c1641333" escape="true">
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>Note </b></i><b><em style="font: inherit;">6</em></b><i><b>. Debt and Other Financing Arrangements:&#xa0;</b></i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>&#xa0;</b></i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> August 1, 2018, </em>the Company entered into a&#xa0;new uncollateralized&#xa0;revolving line-of-credit and term loan governed by a Credit Agreement (the Credit Agreement). The Credit Agreement provides for a revolving credit facility of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739482" contextRef="i_2018-08-01_CreditFacilityAxis-RevolvingCreditFacilityMember_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityMaximumBorrowingCapacity" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-8">600.0</ix:nonFraction> million, which can be increased by an additional $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739483" contextRef="i_2018-08-01_CreditFacilityAxis-RevolvingCreditFacilityMember_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="tech:LineOfCreditFacilityAdditionalBorrowingCapacity" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-8">200.0</ix:nonFraction> million subject to certain conditions, and a term loan of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739484" contextRef="i_2018-08-01_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember_LongtermDebtTypeAxis-TermLoanMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-7">250.0</ix:nonFraction> million. Borrowings under the Credit Agreement <em style="font: inherit;"> may </em>be used for working capital and expenditures of the Company and its subsidiaries, including financing permitted acquisitions. Borrowings under the Credit Agreement bear interest at a variable rate. The current outstanding debt is based on the Eurodollar Loans term for which the interest rate is calculated as the sum of LIBOR plus an applicable margin. The applicable margin is determined for the total leverage ratio of the Company and updated on a quarterly basis. The annualized fee for any unused portion of the credit facility is currently <ix:nonFraction id="c64739485" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_CreditFacilityAxis-RevolvingCreditFacilityMember_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityUnusedCapacityCommitmentFeePercentage" scale="-4" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="4">15</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;basis points. The Company has recorded $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739486" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_CreditFacilityAxis-RevolvingCreditFacilityMember_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:LinesOfCreditCurrent" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">12.5</ix:nonFraction> million of our outstanding borrowings under the Credit Agreement&#xa0;as a&#xa0;current liability&#xa0;in our Consolidated Balance sheet, which represents our required&#xa0;quarterly debt payments to be made in fiscal year <em style="font: inherit;">2021.</em></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Credit Agreement matures on <em style="font: inherit;"> August 1, 2023 </em>and contains customary restrictive and financial covenants and customary events of default. At the closing on <em style="font: inherit;"> August 1, 2018 </em>the company borrowed $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739488" contextRef="d_2018-08-01_2018-08-01_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember_LongtermDebtTypeAxis-TermLoanMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromIssuanceOfLongTermDebt" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-7">250.0</ix:nonFraction> million under the term loan and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739489" contextRef="d_2018-08-01_2018-08-01_CreditFacilityAxis-RevolvingCreditFacilityMember_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ProceedsFromLongTermLinesOfCredit" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-7">330.0</ix:nonFraction> million under the revolving credit facility. As of <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> the outstanding balance under the Credit Agreement was $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739491" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:LongTermDebt" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">357</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739492" contextRef="i_2019-06-30_DebtInstrumentAxis-CreditAgreementMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:LongTermDebt" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">505.2</ix:nonFraction> million respectively.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>

  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" name="us-gaap:LesseeOperatingLeasesTextBlock" id="c1641334" escape="true">
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>Note <em style="font: inherit;">7.</em> Leases:</b></i>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">As a lessee, the company leases offices, labs, and manufacturing facilities, as well as vehicles, copiers, and other equipment. The Company adopted ASU <em style="font: inherit;">No.</em> <em style="font: inherit;">2016</em>-<em style="font: inherit;">02</em> and related standards (collectively ASC <em style="font: inherit;">842,</em> <i>Leases</i>), which replaced previous lease accounting guidance, on <em style="font: inherit;"> July 1, 2019.&#xa0;</em></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><br /> The Company recognizes operating lease expense on a straight-line basis over the lease term. Operating lease right-of-use assets and liabilities are recognized at commencement date based on the present value of lease payments over the lease term. The discount rate used to calculate present value is the Company&#x2019;s incremental borrowing rate or, if available, the rate implicit in the lease. The Company&#xa0;determines the incremental borrowing rate for each lease based primarily on its lease term and the economic environment of the applicable country or region. During fiscal year <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Company recognized $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739499" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:VariableLeaseCost" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.4</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million in variable lease expense&#xa0;in the Consolidated Statements of Earnings and Comprehensive Income.&#xa0; During fiscal year <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Company also recognized $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739501" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:FixedLeaseCost" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">13.0</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million&#xa0;relating to fixed lease expense&#xa0;in the&#xa0;Consolidated Statements of Earnings and Comprehensive Income.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The following table summarizes the balance sheet classification of the Company&#x2019;s operating leases, amounts of right of use assets and lease liabilities,&#xa0;the weighted average remaining lease term, and the weighted average discount rate for the Company&#x2019;s operating leases (asset and liability amounts are in thousands):</p>
   <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" name="tech:LesseeOperatingLeaseAssetAndLiabilitiesTableTextBlock" id="c1641335" escape="true">

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:center;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">Certain leases include <em style="font: inherit;">one</em> or more options to renew, with terms that extend the lease term up to <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c64739505">five</span> years. The Company includes option to renew the lease as part of the right of use lease asset and liability when it is reasonably certain the Company will exercise the option. In addition, certain leases contain fair value purchase and termination options with an associated penalty. In general, the Company&#xa0;is <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> reasonably certain to exercise such options.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>Note </b></i><b><em style="font: inherit;">8.</em></b><i><b> </b></i><i><b>Supplemental Equity and&#xa0;</b></i><i><b>Accumulated Other Comprehensive Income (loss):&#xa0;</b></i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>&#xa0;</b></i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Supplemental Equity </i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company has declared cash dividends per share of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739546" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:CommonStockDividendsPerShareDeclared" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">1.28</ix:nonFraction> in each of the full fiscal&#xa0;years ended&#xa0;&#xa0;<em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em><em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2019, </em>and <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2018. </em>During the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020 </em>and <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2019, </em>the Company repurchased <ix:nonFraction id="c64739547" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockRepurchasedDuringPeriodShares" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">279,381</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;shares at an average share price of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739548" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="tech:StockRepurchasedDuringPeriodAverageSharePrice" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">179.37</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;and <ix:nonFraction id="c64739549" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockRepurchasedDuringPeriodShares" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">95,000</ix:nonFraction> shares at an average share price of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739550" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="tech:StockRepurchasedDuringPeriodAverageSharePrice" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">162.15</ix:nonFraction>, respectively. The Company's accounting policy is to record the portion of&#xa0;share repurchases in excess of the par value&#xa0;entirely in retained earnings. During fiscal year <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> the Company recorded&#xa0;($<ix:nonFraction id="c64739552" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:SurrenderAndRetirementOfStockToExerciseOptionsValue" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" sign="-">0.4</ix:nonFraction>) million within the Consolidated Statements of Shareholders' Equity for the surrender and retirement of stock to exercise option due to net settlement stock options exercises.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>

<tr style="vertical-align: bottom; background-color: rgb(204, 238, 255);"><td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"> <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; margin-left: 18pt;">Earnings per share &#x2013; diluted</p> </td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">$</td><td style="width: 13%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;"><ix:nonFraction id="c64739657" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:EarningsPerShareDiluted" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">5.82</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">$</td><td style="width: 13%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;"><ix:nonFraction id="c64739658" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:EarningsPerShareDiluted" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">2.47</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">$</td><td style="width: 13%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;"><ix:nonFraction id="c64739659" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USDPerShare" name="us-gaap:EarningsPerShareDiluted" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">3.31</ix:nonFraction></td><td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Basic net income per common share is calculated based on the weighted average number of common shares outstanding during the period. Diluted net income per common share is computed by dividing net income by the weighted average number of common and potentially dilutive common shares outstanding during the period. Potentially dilutive common shares of our stock result from dilutive common stock options and restricted stock units. We use the treasury stock method to calculate the weighted-average shares used in the diluted earnings per share computation. Under the treasury stock method, the proceeds from exercise of an option, the amount of compensation cost, if any, for future service that we have <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> yet recognized, and the amount of estimated tax benefits that would be recorded in paid-in capital, if any, when the option is exercised are assumed to be used to repurchase shares in the current period.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The dilutive effect of stock options in the above table excludes all options for which the aggregate exercise proceeds exceeded the average market price for the period. The number of potentially dilutive option shares excluded from the calculation was <ix:nonFraction id="c64739615" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:AntidilutiveSecuritiesExcludedFromComputationOfEarningsPerShareAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.9</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, <ix:nonFraction id="c64739616" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:AntidilutiveSecuritiesExcludedFromComputationOfEarningsPerShareAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.3</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, and <ix:nonFraction id="c64739617" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:AntidilutiveSecuritiesExcludedFromComputationOfEarningsPerShareAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.9</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million for the fiscal years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2019</em>&#xa0;and <em style="font: inherit;">2018,</em> respectively.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><b>&#xa0;</b></p>
   <ix:exclude>

  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" name="us-gaap:DisclosureOfCompensationRelatedCostsShareBasedPaymentsTextBlock" id="c1641343" escape="true">
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>Note </b></i><b><em style="font: inherit;">10.</em></b><i><b>&#xa0;Share-based Compensation and Other Benefit Plans:</b></i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&#xa0;</i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The cost of employee services received in exchange for the award of equity instruments is based on the fair value of the award at the date of grant. Compensation cost is recognized using a straight-line method over the vesting period and is net of estimated forfeitures. Stock option exercises and stock awards are satisfied through the issuance of new shares.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&#xa0;</i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Equity incentive plan:</i> The Company's Second Amended and Restated <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Equity Incentive Plan (the Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan) provides for the granting of incentive and nonqualified stock options, restricted stock, restricted stock units, performance shares, performance units and stock appreciation rights. There are <ix:nonFraction id="c64739663" contextRef="i_2010-06-30_PlanNameAxis-EquityIncentivePlan2010Member" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">7.5</ix:nonFraction> million shares of common stock authorized for grant under the Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan. At <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em>there were <ix:nonFraction id="c64739665" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_PlanNameAxis-EquityIncentivePlan2010Member" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAvailableForGrant" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.9</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million shares of common stock available for grant under the&#xa0;Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan. The maximum term of incentive options granted under the Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan is <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c64739668">ten</span> years. The Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan amended and restated the Company's Amended and Restate <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Equity Incentive Plan (the A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan). The A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan&#xa0;replaced the Company's <em style="font: inherit;">1998</em> Nonqualified Stock Option Plan (the <em style="font: inherit;">1998</em> Plan). The Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan and the <em style="font: inherit;">1998</em> Plan (collectively, the Plans) are administered by the Board of Directors and its Executive Compensation Committee, which determine the persons who are to receive awards under the Plans, the number of shares subject to each award and the term and exercise price of each award. The number of shares of common stock subject to outstanding awards as of&#xa0; <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020 </em>under the Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan were <ix:nonFraction id="c64739678" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_PlanNameAxis-EquityIncentivePlan2010Member" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingNumber" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.6</ix:nonFraction> million. For <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2019 </em>under the Second A&amp;R <em style="font: inherit;">2010</em> Plan and the <em style="font: inherit;">1998</em> Plan were <ix:nonFraction id="c64739681" contextRef="i_2019-06-30_PlanNameAxis-EquityIncentivePlan2010Member" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingNumber" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.6</ix:nonFraction> million and <ix:nonFraction id="c64739682" contextRef="i_2019-06-30_PlanNameAxis-NonqualifiedStockOptionsPlan1998Member" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingNumber" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">20,000</ix:nonFraction>, respectively.&#xa0; On <em style="font: inherit;"> April 26, 2018 </em>the Executive Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors approved a modification to the Plans.&#xa0;&#xa0; The modification implements a new retirement policy that permits retirees to continue vesting in certain time-based stock options granted during employment, resulting in accelerated stock compensation expense for those employees meeting the definition of retirement eligible.&#xa0; This modification resulted in an additional $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739683" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardAcceleratedCompensationCost" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">8.3</ix:nonFraction> million of expense during fiscal year <em style="font: inherit;">2018</em> and affected&#xa0;all employees who participate in the plan.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The fair values of options granted under the Plans were estimated on the date of grant using the Black-Scholes option-pricing model with the following assumptions used:</p>

   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The dividend yield is based on the Company's historical annual cash dividend divided by the market value of the Company's common stock. The expected annualized volatility is based on the Company's historical stock price over a period equivalent to the expected life of the option granted. The risk-free interest rate is based on U.S. Treasury constant maturity interest rates with a term consistent with the expected life of the options granted.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   </ix:continuation>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The total fair value of restricted stock units that vested was $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739713" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockUnitsRSUMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsVestedInPeriodTotalFairValue" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">5.7</ix:nonFraction> million for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739715" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockUnitsRSUMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsVestedInPeriodTotalFairValue" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.1</ix:nonFraction> million for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739717" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockUnitsRSUMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsVestedInPeriodTotalFairValue" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.6</ix:nonFraction> million for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2018.</em>&#xa0;The restricted stock units vest over a <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c64739719">three</span>-year period.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Stock-based compensation cost of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739720" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_IncomeStatementLocationAxis-SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpensesMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">32.4</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739721" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30_IncomeStatementLocationAxis-SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpensesMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">32.3</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739722" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30_IncomeStatementLocationAxis-SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpensesMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">28.2</ix:nonFraction> million&#xa0;was included in selling, general and administrative expense in fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em>&#xa0;and <em style="font: inherit;">2018,</em> respectively. Additionally,&#xa0;Stock-based compensation costs of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739726" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_IncomeStatementLocationAxis-CostOfSalesMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.6</ix:nonFraction> million was included in cost of goods sold in <em style="font: inherit;">2020.</em> As of <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em>there was $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739728" contextRef="i_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:EmployeeServiceShareBasedCompensationNonvestedAwardsTotalCompensationCostNotYetRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">25.3</ix:nonFraction> million of unrecognized compensation cost related to non-vested stock options, non-vested restricted stock units and non-vested restricted stock which will be expensed in fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2021</em>&#xa0;through <em style="font: inherit;">2023</em>&#xa0;using a <ix:nonFraction id="c64739731" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="Pure" name="tech:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNonvestedForfeitureRate" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2">3</ix:nonFraction>% forfeiture rate. The weighted average period over which the compensation cost is expected to be recognized is <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" name="us-gaap:EmployeeServiceShareBasedCompensationNonvestedAwardsTotalCompensationCostNotYetRecognizedPeriodForRecognition1" id="c64739732" format="ixt-sec:duryear">1.9</ix:nonNumeric> years.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Employee stock purchase plan:&#xa0;</i>In fiscal year <em style="font: inherit;">2015,</em> the Company established the Bio-Techne Corporation <em style="font: inherit;">2014</em> Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), which was approved by the Company's shareholders on <em style="font: inherit;"> October 30, 2014, </em>and which is designed to comply with IRS provisions governing employee stock purchase plans. <ix:nonFraction id="c64739735" contextRef="i_2014-10-30_PlanNameAxis-EmployeeStockPurchasePlan2014Member" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">200,000</ix:nonFraction> shares were allocated to the ESPP. The Company recorded expense of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739736" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_PlanNameAxis-EmployeeStockPurchasePlan2014Member" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.4</ix:nonFraction> million, $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739737" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30_PlanNameAxis-EmployeeStockPurchasePlan2014Member" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.5</ix:nonFraction> million,&#xa0;and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739738" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30_PlanNameAxis-EmployeeStockPurchasePlan2014Member" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:AllocatedShareBasedCompensationExpense" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million&#xa0;for the ESPP in fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2018,</em>&#xa0;respectively.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Profit sharing and savings plans: </i>The Company has profit sharing and savings plans for its U.S. employees, which conform to IRS provisions for <em style="font: inherit;">401</em>(k) plans. The Company makes matching contributions to the Plan. The Company has recorded an expense for contributions to the plans of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739743" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_RetirementPlanTypeAxis-USProfitSharingAndSavingsPlanMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanCostRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.2</ix:nonFraction> million, $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739744" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30_RetirementPlanTypeAxis-USProfitSharingAndSavingsPlanMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanCostRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">2.8</ix:nonFraction> million, and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739745" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30_RetirementPlanTypeAxis-USProfitSharingAndSavingsPlanMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanCostRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">2.5</ix:nonFraction> million&#xa0;for the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2018,</em> respectively. The Company operates defined contribution pension plans for its U.K. employees. The Company has recorded an expense for contributions to the plans of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739748" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30_RetirementPlanTypeAxis-GBProfitSharingAndSavingsPlanMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DefinedContributionPlanCostRecognized" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">1.4</ix:nonFraction> million for each of&#xa0;the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2018.</em></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&#xa0;</i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>Performance incentive programs: </i>In fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2020,</em> under certain employment agreements and a Management Incentive Plan available to executive officers and certain management personnel, the Company recorded cash bonuses of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739752" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_PlanNameAxis-PerformanceIncentiveProgramsMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-ExecutiveOfficerMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OfficersCompensation" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">10.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, granted options for <ix:nonFraction id="c64739753" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_PlanNameAxis-PerformanceIncentiveProgramsMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-ExecutiveOfficerMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsGrantsInPeriod" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">751,499</ix:nonFraction> shares of common stock, issued <ix:nonFraction id="c64739754" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockMember_PlanNameAxis-PerformanceIncentiveProgramsMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-ExecutiveOfficerMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesRestrictedStockAwardGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">15,398</ix:nonFraction> restricted common shares and <ix:nonFraction id="c64739755" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_AwardTypeAxis-RestrictedStockUnitsRSUMember_PlanNameAxis-PerformanceIncentiveProgramsMember_TitleOfIndividualAxis-ExecutiveOfficerMember" unitRef="Share" name="us-gaap:StockIssuedDuringPeriodSharesRestrictedStockAwardGross" scale="0" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF">30,858</ix:nonFraction> restricted stock units.    </ix:continuation>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company's effective income tax rate for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> was <ix:nonFraction id="c64739949" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateContinuingOperations" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="3">17.1</ix:nonFraction>% for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> vs <ix:nonFraction id="c64739951" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateContinuingOperations" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="3">14.2</ix:nonFraction>% in the prior year. The change in the effective tax rate for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> were driven by the changes in the net discrete tax benefits $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739954" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitDiscreteTaxItems" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">19.4</ix:nonFraction> million&#xa0;and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739955" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitDiscreteTaxItems" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">12.7</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company&#x2019;s effective income tax rate for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em>&#xa0;was <ix:nonFraction id="c64739957" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateContinuingOperations" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="3">14.2</ix:nonFraction>% vs (<ix:nonFraction id="c64739958" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateContinuingOperations" scale="-2" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="3" sign="-">0.2</ix:nonFraction>%) in the prior year. The change in the effective tax rate for fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2018</em> was driven by changes in net discrete tax benefits of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739961" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitDiscreteTaxItems" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">12.7</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739962" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitDiscreteTaxItems" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">34.4</ix:nonFraction> million for fiscal year <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2018,</em> respectively.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <div style="text-align: justify; font-size: 10pt;">
     The Company's discrete tax benefits&#xa0;in fiscal
    <em style="font: inherit;">2020</em> primarily related to share-based compensation excess tax benefits of
    $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739966" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ExcessTaxBenefitFromShareBasedCompensationOperatingActivities" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">17.7</ix:nonFraction> million.
   </div>
   <div style="text-align: justify; font-size: 10pt;">
     &#xa0;
   </div>
   <div style="text-align: justify; font-size: 10pt;">
     The Company's discrete tax benefits in fiscal
    <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em> primarily related to share-based compensation excess tax benefits of
    $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739968" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ExcessTaxBenefitFromShareBasedCompensationOperatingActivities" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">7.2</ix:nonFraction> million,&#xa0;
    $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739969" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitDiscreteTaxItemsAcquisitions" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" sign="-">3.2</ix:nonFraction> million related to fiscal
    <em style="font: inherit;">2019</em>&#xa0;acquisitions, and
    $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739971" contextRef="d_2018-07-01_2019-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitDiscreteTaxItemsStateApportionments" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">2.0</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million&#xa0;for tax refunds relating to certain&#xa0;state&#xa0;apportionments. The prior fiscal&#xa0;year was benefited from acquisition payments made to employees and
    <em style="font: inherit;">third</em> parties, which were deductible for tax purposes.&#xa0;
   </div>
   <div style="text-align: justify; font-size: 10pt;">
     &#xa0;
   </div>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman; font-size: 10pt;">In fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2018,</em> the Company recognized net discrete tax benefits of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739974" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitDiscreteTaxItems" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">34.4</ix:nonFraction> million. The primary driver in fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2018</em> discrete tax benefits was&#xa0;a&#xa0;discrete net tax benefit of&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c64739976" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateReconciliationTaxCutsAndJobsActOf2017Amount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">33.0</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million related to the Tax Act (as described further below).&#xa0;This net tax benefit consisted of&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c64739977" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitContinuingOperationsAdjustmentOfDeferredTaxAssetLiability" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">36.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million due to the re-measurement of the Company&#x2019;s deferred tax accounts to reflect the U.S. federal corporate tax rate reduction impact to our net deferred tax balances offset by expense for the federal transition tax of&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c64739978" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitFederalTranslationTax" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">3.3</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million. Also impacting the Company&#x2019;s fiscal&#xa0;<em style="font: inherit;">2018</em>&#xa0;effective tax rate was a&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c64739980" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:IncomeTaxExpenseBenefitStockOptionsExercises" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">2.2</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million tax benefit related to stock option exercises offset by a net discrete tax expense of&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c64739981" contextRef="d_2017-07-01_2018-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="tech:EffectiveIncomeTaxRateReconciliationNondeductibleExpenseRevaluationOfContingentConsideration" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">4.2</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million related to the revaluation of contingent consideration, which is&#xa0;<em style="font: inherit;">not</em>&#xa0;a tax deductible expens</span>e.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">On <em style="font: inherit;"> December 22, 2017, </em>the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (the &#x201c;Tax Act&#x201d;) was enacted, which reduced the U.S. federal corporate tax rate from <em style="font: inherit;">35%</em> to <em style="font: inherit;">21%,</em> required companies to pay a <em style="font: inherit;">one</em>-time transition tax on earnings of certain foreign subsidiaries that were previously tax deferred and created new taxes on certain foreign sourced earnings. The Tax Act added many new provisions including changes the deduction for executive compensation, a tax on global intangible low taxed income (&#x201c;GILTI&#x201d;), the base erosion anti abuse tax (&#x201c;BEAT&#x201d;) and a deduction for foreign derived intangible income (&#x201c;FDII&#x201d;).</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company continues to monitor newly enacted regulations, clarifications, and changes in guidance&#xa0; the&#xa0;&#x201c;Tax Act&#x201d;, which was enacted on&#xa0;<em style="font: inherit;"> December 22, 2017.&#xa0;</em>The Company recognizes changes in legislation&#xa0;in the period&#xa0;enacted, which <em style="font: inherit;"> may&#xa0;</em>have a material impact on our effective tax rate in future periods.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   </ix:exclude>
   <ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">A deferred tax valuation allowance is required when it is more likely than <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> that all or a portion of deferred tax assets will <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be realized. The valuation allowance as of <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020&#xa0;</em>was $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739990" contextRef="i_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsValuationAllowance" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">7.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million compared to $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739991" contextRef="i_2019-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsValuationAllowance" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">7.0</ix:nonFraction> million in the prior year.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">As of <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em>the $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739992" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:ValuationAllowanceDeferredTaxAssetChangeInAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">7.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million valuation allowance relates to certain foreign and state tax net operating loss and state credit carryforwards that existed at the date the Company acquired Quad, Exosome, ACD, Novus, ProteinSimple and CyVek as well as immaterial amounts generated after the acquisitions. The Company believes it is more likely than <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> that these tax carryovers will <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> be realized.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; text-align: justify;">As of <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em>the Company has federal operating loss carryforwards of approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739995" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis-DomesticCountryMember_IncomeTaxAuthorityNameAxis-InternalRevenueServiceIRSMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">64.2</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million and state operating loss carryforwards of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739996" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis-StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">130.6</ix:nonFraction> million from its acquisitions of Quad, Exosome, ACD, ProteinSimple and CyVek, which are <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> limited under IRC Section <em style="font: inherit;">382.</em>&#xa0;As of <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em>the Company has foreign net operating loss carryforwards of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64739999" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis-ForeignCountryMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">13.7</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million.&#xa0;The net operating loss carryforwards expire between fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2021</em>&#xa0;and <em style="font: inherit;">2036.</em> The Company has a deferred tax asset of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64740002" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_ValuationAllowanceByDeferredTaxAssetAxis-OperatingLossCarryforwardsMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsNet" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">20.2</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, net of the valuation allowance discussed above, related to the net operating loss carryovers. As of <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em>the Company has federal and state tax credit carryforwards of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64740003" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis-DomesticCountryMember_IncomeTaxAuthorityNameAxis-InternalRevenueServiceIRSMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:TaxCreditCarryforwardAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">5.0</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c64740004" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis-StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:TaxCreditCarryforwardAmount" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">5.7</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, respectively. The federal tax credit carryforwards expire between <em style="font: inherit;">2028</em>&#xa0;and <em style="font: inherit;">2038.</em> The majority of the state credit carryforwards have <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> expiry date. The Company has a deferred tax asset of $<ix:nonFraction id="c64740008" contextRef="i_2020-06-30_ValuationAllowanceByDeferredTaxAssetAxis-TaxCreditCarryforwardsMember" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:DeferredTaxAssetsNet" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5">7.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, net of the valuation allowance discussed above, related to the tax credit carryovers.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; text-align: justify;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; text-align: justify;">The Company has <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> recognized a deferred tax liability for unremitted foreign earnings of approximately $<ix:nonFraction id="c64740010" contextRef="i_2020-06-30" unitRef="USD" name="us-gaap:UndistributedEarningsOfForeignSubsidiaries" scale="6" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6">186</ix:nonFraction> million from its foreign operations because its subsidiaries have invested or will invest the undistributed earnings indefinitely. The transition tax included as part of the Tax Act resulted&#xa0;in the previously untaxed foreign earnings being included in the federal and state fiscal <em style="font: inherit;">2018</em>&#xa0;taxable income.&#xa0; The <em style="font: inherit;">one</em>-time transition tax was based on certain foreign earnings for which earnings have been previously indefinitely reinvested as well as the amount of earnings held in cash and other specified assets.&#xa0; &#xa0;<em style="font: inherit;">No</em> additional income taxes have been provided for cumulative unremitted foreign earnings as&#xa0;at this time&#xa0;our intention with respect to unremitted foreign earnings is to continue to indefinitely reinvest outside the U.S. those earnings needed for working capital or additional foreign investment. If there are policy changes, we would record applicable taxes at that time.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; text-align: justify;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt; text-align: justify;">We continue to analyze our global working capital requirements and the potential tax liabilities that would be incurred if the non-U.S. subsidiaries distribute cash to the U.S. parent, which include local country withholding tax and potential U.S. state taxation.&#xa0; In addition, we anticipate that further guidance from the IRS and US Treasury related to the Tax Act could impact the amount of any related taxes.&#xa0; Therefore, it is <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> practical to estimate the amount of the deferred income tax liabilities related to investments in these foreign subsidiaries.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt 7.2pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The following is a reconciliation of the beginning and ending balance of unrecognized tax benefits (in thousands):</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfUnrecognizedTaxBenefitsRollForwardTableTextBlock" id="c1641353" escape="true">

   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company does <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> believe it is reasonably possible that the total amounts of unrecognized tax benefits will significantly increase in the next <em style="font: inherit;">twelve</em> months. The Company files income tax returns in the U.S. federal and certain state tax jurisdictions, and several jurisdictions outside the U.S. The Company's federal returns are subject to tax assessment for <em style="font: inherit;">2017</em>&#xa0;and subsequent years. State and foreign income tax returns are generally subject to examination for a period of <em style="font: inherit;">three</em> to <em style="font: inherit;">five</em> years after filing of the respective return. The state impact of any federal changes remains subject to examination by various states for a period of up to <em style="font: inherit;">one</em> year after formal notification to the states.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>

       <em style="font: inherit;">62</em>

  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" name="us-gaap:SegmentReportingDisclosureTextBlock" id="c1641354" escape="true">
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>Note </b></i><b><em style="font: inherit;">12.</em></b><i><b> Segment Information:</b></i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company operates&#xa0;under&#xa0;<ix:nonFraction id="c64740156" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30" unitRef="Pure" name="us-gaap:NumberOfReportableSegments" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="INF">two</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;operating segments, Protein Sciences and Diagnostics and Genomics.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company's Protein Sciences segment is comprised of the Reagent Sol<span style="background-color:null;">utions division and Analytical Solution division. These businesses manufacture consumables used for conducting laboratory experiments by both industry and academic scientists within the biotechnology and biomedical life science fields. <ix:nonFraction id="c64740157" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis-SalesRevenueSegmentMember_ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis-CustomerConcentrationRiskMember_StatementBusinessSegmentsAxis-ProteinSciencesMember" unitRef="Pure" name="tech:NumberOfMajorCustomers" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">No</ix:nonFraction> customer in the Protein Sciences&#xa0;segment accounted for more than <em style="font: inherit;">10%</em> of the segment&#x2019;s net sales for the years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020</em></span>, <em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2018.</em></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company's Diagnostics and&#xa0;Genomics is comprised of the Diagnostics Reagents division and the&#xa0;Genomics division. The Diagnostics Reagents division develops and manufactures a range of controls and calibrators used with diagnostic equipment and as proficiency testing tools, as well as other reagents incorporated into diagnostic kits. The Genomics division consists of&#xa0;Genomics and Exosome products and sells a portfolio of clinical molecular diagnostic oncology assays, as well as tissue-based in-situ hybridization assays for research in clinical use. <ix:nonFraction id="c64740161" contextRef="d_2019-07-01_2020-06-30_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis-SalesRevenueSegmentMember_ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis-CustomerConcentrationRiskMember_StatementBusinessSegmentsAxis-DiagnosticsAndGenomicsMember" unitRef="Pure" name="tech:NumberOfMajorCustomers" scale="0" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="-3">No</ix:nonFraction> customer in the Diagnostics&#xa0;and Genomics segment accounted for more than <em style="font: inherit;">10%</em> of the segment&#x2019;s net sales for the fiscal years ended <em style="font: inherit;"> June 30, 2020, </em><em style="font: inherit;">2019,</em> and <em style="font: inherit;">2018.</em>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">There are <em style="font: inherit;">no</em> concentrations of business transacted with a particular customer or supplier or concentrations of revenue from a particular product or geographic area that would severely impact the Company in the near term.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Following is financial information relating to the operating segments (in thousands):</p>

   <ix:exclude>&#xa0;</ix:exclude>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company has some integrated facilities that serve multiple segments. As such, asset and capital expenditure information by operating segment has <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> been provided and is <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> available, since the Company does <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> produce or utilize such information internally. In addition, although depreciation and amortization expense is a component of each operating&#xa0;segment&#x2019;s operating results, it is <em style="font: inherit;">not</em> discretely identifiable.</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">The Company has disclosed sales by&#xa0;geographic area&#xa0;based on the location of the customer or distributor in Note <em style="font: inherit;">2.</em> The Company has disclosed dis-aggregated product and service revenue&#xa0;by consumables, instruments, and services in Note <em style="font: inherit;">2.</em> The Company considers total instrument&#xa0;and total service revenue to represent&#xa0;similar groups of products in&#xa0;the fiscal years presented. The Company considered our consumables sold in&#xa0;the Protein Sciences and Diagnostics and Genomics segments&#xa0;to represent different groups of products and therefore have separately&#xa0;disclosed the related consumables revenue (in thousands) :</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">Long-lived assets are comprised of land, buildings and improvements and equipment, net of accumulated depreciation and other assets.</p>

   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>Note </b></i><b><em style="font: inherit;">13.</em></b><i><b> Quarterly Financial Data (unaudited):</b></i></p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: justify; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i><b>&#xa0;</b></i></p>

   <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;"><i>&#xa0;</i></p>

  <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt 7.2pt; text-align: center;"><b>Report of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm</b></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">To the Shareholders and Board of Directors</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">Bio-Techne Corporation:</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;"><i>Opinion on the Consolidated Financial Statements</i></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">We have audited the accompanying consolidated balance sheets of Bio-Techne Corporation and&#xa0;subsidiaries (the Company) as of June 30, 2020 and 2019, the related consolidated statements of earnings and comprehensive income, shareholders&#x2019; equity, and cash flows for each of the years in the three-year period ended June 30, 2020, and the related notes (collectively, the consolidated financial statements). In our opinion, the consolidated financial statements present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of the Company as of June 30, 2020 and 2019, and the results of its operations and its cash flows for each of the years in the three-year period ended June 30, 2020, in conformity with U.S.&#xa0;generally accepted accounting principles.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">We also have audited, in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United States) (PCAOB), the Company&#x2019;s internal control over financial reporting as of June 30, 2020, based on criteria established in <i>Internal Control &#x2013; Integrated Framework (2013)</i> issued by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission, and our report dated August 26, 2020 expressed an unqualified opinion on the effectiveness of the Company&#x2019;s internal control over financial reporting.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;"><i>Change in Accounting Principle </i></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">As discussed in Note 1 to the consolidated financial statements, the Company has changed its method of accounting for revenue as of July 1, 2018, due to the adoption of Accounting Standards Update 2014-09, <i>Revenue from Contracts with Customers </i>(Topic 606), and related amendments.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">As discussed in Note 1 to the consolidated financial statements, the Company has changed its method of accounting for Leases as of July 1, 2019, due to the adoption of Accounting Standards Update 2016-02, <i>Leases </i>(Topic 842) and related amendments.&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;"><i>Basis for Opinion</i></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">These consolidated financial statements are the responsibility of the Company&#x2019;s management. Our responsibility is to express an opinion on these consolidated financial statements based on our audits. We are a public accounting firm registered with the PCAOB and are required to be independent with respect to the Company in accordance with the U.S. federal securities laws and the applicable rules and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission and the PCAOB.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">We conducted our audits in accordance with the standards of the PCAOB. Those standards require that we plan and perform the audit to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the consolidated financial statements are free of material misstatement, whether due to error or fraud. Our audits included performing procedures to assess the risks of material misstatement of the consolidated financial statements, whether due to error or fraud, and performing procedures that respond to those risks. Such procedures included examining, on a test basis, evidence regarding the amounts and disclosures in the consolidated financial statements. Our audits also included evaluating the accounting principles used and significant estimates made by management, as well as evaluating the overall presentation of the consolidated financial statements. We believe that our audits provide a reasonable basis for our opinion.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;"><i>Critical Audit Matter</i></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">The critical audit matter communicated below is a matter arising from the current period audit of the consolidated financial statements that was communicated or required to be communicated to the audit committee and that: (1) relates to accounts or disclosures that are material to the consolidated financial statements and (2) involved our especially challenging, subjective, or complex judgments. The communication of a critical audit matter does not alter in any way our opinion on the consolidated financial statements, taken as a whole, and we are not, by communicating the critical audit matter below, providing a separate opinion on the critical audit matter or on the accounts or disclosures to which it&#xa0;relates.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;"><i>&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;</i></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;"><i>&#xa0; Goodwill impairment analysis for the Exosome reporting unit </i></p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">As discussed in Note 1 to the consolidated financial statements, the goodwill balance as of June 30, 2020 was $728.3 million, of which $105.4 million related to the Exosome reporting unit. The Company performs goodwill impairment testing on an annual basis and whenever events or changes in circumstances indicate that the carrying value of a reporting unit likely exceeds its fair value. This involves estimating the fair value of the reporting units using discounted cash flow models.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">We identified the evaluation of the goodwill impairment analysis for the Exosome reporting unit as a critical audit matter. There was a high degree of subjectivity in applying and evaluating certain key assumptions used in the discounted cash flow model to estimate the fair value of the Exosome reporting unit. Specifically, the revenue growth rates and the discount rate were challenging to test as they represented subjective determinations of future market and economic conditions. Changes to those assumptions could have had a significant effect on the Company&#x2019;s assessment of the fair&#xa0;value of the goodwill.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">The following are the primary procedures we performed to address this critical audit matter. We evaluated the design and tested the operating effectiveness of certain internal controls related to the critical audit matter. This included controls related to the Company&#x2019;s determination of the estimated fair value of the Exosome reporting unit, including controls related to the:</p>
  <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">
   <tbody>
    <tr>
     <td style="width:38pt;">&#xa0;</td>
     <td style="width:10pt;vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">&#x25cf;</p> </td>
     <td style="vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">development of revenue growth rates</p> </td>
    </tr>

   </tbody>
  </table>
  <table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">
   <tbody>
    <tr>
     <td style="width:38pt;">&#xa0;</td>
     <td style="width:10pt;vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">&#x25cf;</p> </td>
     <td style="vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">selection of the discount rate</p> </td>
    </tr>

   </tbody>
  </table>
  <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">We performed sensitivity analyses over the revenue growth rate and discount rate assumptions to assess their impact on the Company&#x2019;s determination that the fair value of the Exosome reporting unit exceeded its carrying value. We evaluated the reasonableness of the Company&#x2019;s forecasted revenue growth rates for the Exosome reporting unit by comparing the growth assumptions to industry benchmarks and other industry related third-party data. We also compared the Company&#x2019;s historical revenue forecasts to actual results to assess the Company&#x2019;s ability to accurately forecast. In addition, we involved valuation professionals with specialized skills and knowledge, who assisted in:</p>
  <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">
   <tbody>
    <tr>
     <td style="width:38pt;">&#xa0;</td>
     <td style="width:10pt;vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">&#x25cf;</p> </td>
     <td style="vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">evaluating the discount rate used in the valuation, by comparing it against a discount rate range that was independently developed using publicly available market data for comparable entities, and</p> </td>
    </tr>

   </tbody>
  </table>
  <table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width:100%;font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;">
   <tbody>
    <tr>
     <td style="width:38pt;">&#xa0;</td>
     <td style="width:10pt;vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">&#x25cf;</p> </td>
     <td style="vertical-align:top;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;margin-right:7.2pt;margin-top:0pt;text-align:left;margin-bottom:0pt;font-size:10pt;">testing the estimate of the Exosome reporting unit&#x2019;s fair value using the reporting unit&#x2019;s cash flow forecast and discount rate, and comparing the result to the Company&#x2019;s fair value estimate.</p> </td>
    </tr>

   </tbody>
  </table>
  <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; margin-bottom: 0pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">We have served as the Company&#x2019;s auditor since 2002.</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">/s/ KPMG LLP</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt 7.2pt;text-align:left;">Minneapolis, Minnesota<br /> August 26, 2020</p>
  <p style="margin: 0pt; text-align: left; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt;">&#xa0;</p>
  <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
   <div class="PGFTR" style="text-align: center; width: 100%">
    <div class="hf-row">
     <div class="hf-cell PGNUM" style="text-align: center; font-size: 10pt; font-family: Times New Roman;">

      <a href="#toc" style="font-weight: initial; font-style: initial; font-variant: initial; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif;">Table of Contents</a>
